note: Both model families were evaluated end-to-end through the same split, calibration, and threshold pipeline on identical synthetic data.

baseline_linear.note: Logistic regression on pixel features learns a well-separated decision boundary on this synthetic task. Temperature < 1.0 indicates mild underconfidence corrected by calibration.

compact_cnn.note: Gradient-descent CNN produces near-uniform output (~0.445 for all cases) on 72 training samples. To reach any sensitivity the threshold is forced to the minimum, classifying everything as positive and collapsing specificity to zero. This is a data-volume failure, not an architecture failure.

tradeoffs: On small synthetic datasets, logistic regression is the appropriate choice. Gradient-descent models require more data, augmentation, and regularization to converge — the compact_cnn result illustrates that model complexity does not substitute for an appropriate data and feature strategy. Both models run through identical evaluation discipline so the comparison is fair.
